app.description: Cloudflare Tunnel creates a secure outbound-only connection from your server to Cloudflare's edge — no open inbound ports on your router required. This community app automatically generates wildcard ingress rules for all domains defined in your .env (DOMAINNAME_1, DOMAINNAME_2, ...), routing all subdomain traffic through Traefik. Your home IP is never exposed in DNS.
app.descriptionShort: Secure outbound tunnel to Cloudflare — no open ports

variables.name: CLOUDFLARE_TUNNEL_TOKEN
variables.description: Tunnel token from Cloudflare Zero Trust → Networks → Tunnels → your tunnel → Configure

messages.text: Cloudflare Tunnel is running. It will create wildcard ingress rules for every DOMAINNAME_N defined in your .env and route all traffic to Traefik on localhost:443.
To add more domains: add DOMAINNAME_2, DOMAINNAME_3, etc. to your .env and restart the container with: docker restart cloudflare-tunnel

Cloudflare enforces a 100 MB request body limit on proxied traffic. For services that transfer large files (Nextcloud, Immich) or live video streams (Frigate), use grey-cloud DNS records that bypass the proxy.
To verify: docker logs cloudflare-tunnel | grep -E 'Tunnel ID|domain|ERROR'
